name: Aiea Bowl Restaurant
tagline: Bowling alley restaurant with surprisingly excellent local food — the oxtail soup draws people from all over.
mustOrder: Oxtail soup — clear broth, tender meat, served with mustard cabbage and rice
Fried noodles — local-style chow mein, crispy and savory
Hamburger steak with gravy — classic plate lunch comfort food
parking: Large parking lot for the bowling alley. Never an issue.
talkStory: Aiea Bowl opened in 1960 and the restaurant inside became famous on its own — locals drive from across the island just for the oxtail soup, a crystal-clear broth with fork-tender meat, mustard cabbage, and fresh ginger. Featured on multiple 'Best of Hawaii' lists despite being inside a bowling alley. The same family has run the kitchen for decades. Go weekday lunch, order the oxtail with extra rice, then roll a few frames after — prices still feel stuck in a previous decade.
